biological_activities.mic_unit: mµmoles per mg (dry weight) of cells
mµmoles of antimycin per mg (dry weight) of cells

mechanism_of_action: Antimycin A inhibits metabolic reduction of cytochrome c, hinders plasma-membrane functions, inhibits concentrative uptake of a-aminoisobutyrate and a-methyl-glucoside, induces loss of inorganic phosphate and other substances from whole cells, inhibits aerobic respiration, and acts as a lytic agent for isolated protoplasts. The lytic process involves association of lipophilic antimycin molecules with the lipid-rich plasma membrane in a lethal reaction, leading to lesions in the osmotic barrier and outflow of small molecules. Binding to protein is also suggested.
